title: Version record production and round-trip conformance suite
purpose: Produce the cross-system version record from a loaded Package so the engine and store reference a workflow version through one shared aion-core type, and provide the conformance suite that proves the write and read paths fit together: a built package loads back intact, builds are reproducible, and integrity rejection actually fires.
task: Add a function producing the aion-core version-record type from a loaded Package (logical entry module, content hash, declared activities, input/output schemas). Then build the round-trip conformance suite: write a package, load it, assert manifest/beams/namespaced-names survive; assert two builds of identical inputs are byte-identical; assert a post-pack alteration of the beams is rejected on load. Do not write the record to any store.

requirements.title: Version record from a loaded Package
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL produce the shared aion-core version-record type from a loaded Package, carrying the logical entry module, the content hash, the declared activities, and the input/output schemas.
acceptance: version.rs defines a function (e.g. Package::version_record()) returning the aion-core version-record type.
The record carries the logical entry module, the content hash, the declared activities, and the input/output schemas drawn from the loaded Package.
The function uses the aion-core type directly (no re-definition of a parallel version type in this crate).
A unit test builds and loads a package, produces its version record, and asserts each field matches the manifest and content hash.

requirements.title: Write/read round-trip suite
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a test suite that builds a package, loads it back, and asserts the manifest, beam set, and namespaced module names survive intact.
acceptance: tests/roundtrip.rs builds a package (non-trivial: multiple beams, declared activities, schemas, with and without source) and loads it via Package::load_from_bytes.
The loaded manifest equals the built manifest (with the version stamped), the loaded BeamSet equals the input BeamSet, and the optional source matches.
The loaded deployed module names equal namespace::deployed_name(logical, content_hash) for every module.
The test is a real runtime test, not gated out or ignored.

requirements.title: Reproducibility and integrity-rejection assertions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL assert that two builds of identical inputs are byte-identical and that a package whose beams are altered after packing fails to load with IntegrityMismatch.
acceptance: A test builds identical inputs twice and asserts the output byte buffers are equal (reproducibility, CO8).
A test takes a valid package's bytes, mutates a byte inside a beam entry (re-zipping or patching the entry), and asserts Package::load returns PackageError::IntegrityMismatch.
A test asserts that including vs omitting source yields the same content hash / version (CO7).
All tests run under cargo test without #[ignore].

boundaries: SHALL NOT write the version record to any store — that is AC (in-memory) / AS (libSQL) / AX (Postgres).
SHALL NOT test VM registration or execution — that is the engine cluster (AE).
SHALL NOT add a parallel version type; it uses the aion-core version-record type.
verification: cargo test -p aion-package passes, including the round-trip suite.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
